summary: Lina did not know the colours or the clothes. The Emma Saturday students were fun. They behaved quite well and sometimes needed to be reminded that they could be in orange instead of green, and it worked wonders. Gabriel knew or at least understood the worksheet activity very well. They all could understand the clean and dirty concept and dry and wet too. They could all repeat the structures well and understood the Emma is wearing… activity.

They all behaved quite well and sometimes needed to be reminded that they could be in orange instead of green, and it worked wonders. Lina did not know the colours or the clothes. Gabriel knew or at least understood the worksheet activity very well. They could all understand the clean and dirty concept, dry and wet too. They could all repeat the structures well and understood the "Emma is wearing..." activity.

They were all a bit loud to begin with, so I needed to put in place the traffic light, and after that they participated super well. They all could tell their age and their gender. They could all repeat the story for story time and seemed to know a few words. They all could put the clothes items on and take them off, and with Robin’s help, Gabriel, Milo and Lina could put the correct coloured items. Lina kept asking over and over again where she needed to place the things when she actually knew the response and it was a tad annoying. She stopped when we told her to because it even annoyed Gabriel. They could all do the worksheet activity very well and could count super well. They could ask for the counters and could understand the corresponding colour and the clothing item on their bingo card. They partially understood the Numbers songs.

Lina did not know the story at all and needed help to tell it. She could tell her age and gender, almost always using the complete structure, and could pair up Emma dirty/clean body parts, but she needed me to remind her of the full structure to use. She was quite distracted and tired today. She could do the worksheet activity and liked the what's missing activity.
They weren’t super attentive. Lina knew the story very well. The class could all clean Emma and dress her, repeating the right structure, but they did not really master the clothing items vocabulary.

Lina needed to pay attention when the teacher was giving workshop instructions because she kept asking 'quoi quoi quoi,' and the teacher got a bit annoyed before she stopped. She needed help with the vocabulary cards: the teacher had to tell her what the word and image were, while the others could pair them up more easily. The group was playful and loud at times but could calm down when reminded. They liked the new story tattoo, were excited to hear more, could place tattoos on the right body part with a little help, and could place stickers on their body parts too.

Gabrielle, Milo and Charlie were absent, so it was just Robin, Lina and Roderick. Lina could pair up the correct actions in the correct rooms. She could also repeat the new vocabulary words and the new structures. She could tell her age and her gender, and she was in a very good mood.
